gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The **type-level opt-in** to lossy transcoding (spec §3 / C1 / G2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " A `Lossy<T>` is the only way to receive a value that was produced by substituting characters"] # [doc = " that were not representable in the target encoding. The `substituted` count and `marker`"] # [doc = " character are always present in the value — the lossiness is **un-droppable** (G2 / C1)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The default replacement character is U+FFFD (the Unicode replacement character). A caller"] # [doc = " who wants a different replacement character passes it explicitly to `*_lossy` operations."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # FLAG — Q1 (Lossy opt-in shape)"] # [doc = " This is a distinct type per spec §7-Q1's proposed disposition. Whether the replacement marker"] # [doc = " should be configurable (as here, via `marker`) vs fixed at U+FFFD is pending maintainer"] # [doc = " decision. The `marker` field exists to anticipate the configurable direction without guessing"] # [doc = " (G2 / VR-5)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Guarantee tag: `Exact` / total"] # [doc = " The `Lossy` wrapper itself carries no accuracy semantics — it is a total, value-semantic"] # [doc = " record of what was substituted. The `substituted` count is `Exact` (an exact integer count"] # [doc = " of the substitutions that occurred)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub struct Lossy < T > { # [doc = " The transcoded/encoded value (with substitutions applied)."] pub value : T , # [doc = " The count of characters that were substituted."] pub substituted : usize , # [doc = " The replacement character that was used (default U+FFFD)."] pub marker : char , }
gaps.reason: struct `Lossy` uses named fields and references a type not resolvable in-file — emitting it positionally would introduce an unresolved reference that poisons the file's `myc check`; left gapped under the M-1006 resolvability gate (VR-5/G2)

gaps.snippet: impl < T > Lossy < T > { # [doc = " Construct a `Lossy<T>` with a given value, substitution count, and marker."] # [must_use] pub fn new (value : T , substituted : usize , marker : char) -> Self { Lossy { value , substituted , marker , } } # [doc = " Construct a `Lossy<T>` using the default U+FFFD replacement marker."] # [must_use] pub fn with_default_marker (value : T , substituted : usize) -> Self { Lossy { value , substituted , marker : '\u{FFFD}' , } } # [doc = " Whether any substitutions occurred (convenience; the caller can also inspect `substituted`)."] # [must_use] pub fn is_lossless (& self) -> bool { self . substituted == 0 } }

gaps.snippet: # [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: * ; # [doc = " `Text::new` round-trips: the content is exactly the input (C4 — exact)."] # [test] fn text_new_round_trips () { let t = Text :: new ("hello") ; assert_eq ! (t . as_str () , "hello") ; } # [doc = " Empty text is empty and reports zero bytes."] # [test] fn text_empty () { let t = Text :: new ("") ; assert ! (t . is_empty ()) ; assert_eq ! (t . len_bytes () , 0) ; } # [doc = " Multi-byte UTF-8 strings are preserved exactly (C4)."] # [test] fn text_new_preserves_multibyte_utf8 () { let s = "café" ; let t = Text :: new (s) ; assert_eq ! (t . as_str () , s) ; assert_eq ! (t . len_bytes () , s . len ()) ; } # [doc = " `Text` equality is content-addressed: two texts with equal bytes are equal (RFC-0001 §4.6 / C4)."] # [test] fn text_equality_is_content_addressed () { let a = Text :: new ("hello") ; let b = Text :: new ("hello") ; assert_eq ! (a , b , "texts with equal content must be equal (C4 / RFC-0001 §4.6)") ; } # [doc = " Texts with different content are not equal."] # [test] fn text_different_content_not_equal () { let a = Text :: new ("hello") ; let b = Text :: new ("world") ; assert_ne ! (a , b) ; } # [doc = " `Display` renders the text content verbatim."] # [test] fn text_display_is_content () { let t = Text :: new ("greet") ; assert_eq ! (t . to_string () , "greet") ; } # [doc = " `From<String>` and `From<&str>` both yield the correct text."] # [test] fn text_from_conversions () { let t1 = Text :: from ("hello") ; let t2 = Text :: from (String :: from ("hello")) ; assert_eq ! (t1 , t2) ; } # [doc = " `AsRef<str>` exposes the underlying string slice."] # [test] fn text_as_ref_str () { let t = Text :: new ("test") ; let s : & str = t . as_ref () ; assert_eq ! (s , "test") ; } # [doc = " `Lossy::with_default_marker` uses U+FFFD (the spec's proposed default marker)."] # [test] fn lossy_default_marker_is_replacement_char () { let l = Lossy :: < Vec < u8 > > :: with_default_marker (vec ! [0x3F] , 2) ; assert_eq ! (l . marker , '\u{FFFD}') ; assert_eq ! (l . substituted , 2) ; } # [doc = " `Lossy::is_lossless` returns true iff `substituted == 0`."] # [test] fn lossy_is_lossless_iff_zero_substitutions () { let lossless = Lossy :: < Vec < u8 > > :: with_default_marker (vec ! [] , 0) ; let lossy = Lossy :: < Vec < u8 > > :: with_default_marker (vec ! [0x3F] , 1) ; assert ! (lossless . is_lossless () , "zero substitutions → lossless") ; assert ! (! lossy . is_lossless () , "one substitution → not lossless") ; } # [doc = " `Lossy` carries the substitution count in the value — it is un-droppable (G2 / C1)."] # [doc = " Guard: a struct with no `substituted` field would make this assertion fail."] # [test] fn lossy_substitution_count_is_in_the_value () { let l = Lossy :: new (vec ! [b'?'] , 3_usize , '?') ; assert_eq ! (l . substituted , 3 , "substitution count must be in the Lossy value (G2 / C1)") ; assert_eq ! (l . marker , '?') ; } # [doc = " Equality: two `Lossy` values are equal iff their `value`, `substituted`, and `marker` are"] # [doc = " equal (content-addressed)."] # [test] fn lossy_equality_considers_all_fields () { let a = Lossy :: new (vec ! [b'x'] , 1_usize , '\u{FFFD}') ; let b = Lossy :: new (vec ! [b'x'] , 1_usize , '\u{FFFD}') ; let c = Lossy :: new (vec ! [b'x'] , 2_usize , '\u{FFFD}') ; assert_eq ! (a , b) ; assert_ne ! (a , c , "different substitution counts must not be equal") ; } }
